questions.questionText: A group of 15 element, which is a metal and forms a hydride with strongest reducing power among group 15 hydrides. The element is :

questions.questionText: If for x $$\in$$ $$\left( {0,{\pi  \over 2}} \right)$$, log<sub>10</sub>sinx + log<sub>10</sub>cosx = $$-$$1 and log<sub>10</sub>(sinx + cosx) = $${1 \over 2}$$(log<sub>10</sub> n $$-$$ 1), n > 0, then the value of n is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The decomposition of formic acid on gold surface follows first order kinetics. If the rate constant at 300 K is 1.0 $$\times$$ 10<sup>$$-$$3</sup> s<sup>$$-$$1</sup> and the activation energy E<sub>a</sub> = 11.488 kJ mol<sup>$$-$$1</sup>, the rate constant at 200 K is ____________ $$\times$$ 10<sup>$$-$$5</sup> s<sup>$$-$$1</sup>. (Round off to the Nearest Integer). (Given : R = 8.314 J mol<sup>$$-$$1</sup> K<sup>$$-$$1</sup>)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Let a vector $$\alpha \widehat i + \beta \widehat j$$ be obtained by rotating the vector $$\sqrt 3 \widehat i + \widehat j$$ by an angle 45$$^\circ$$ about the origin in counterclockwise direction in the first quadrant. Then the area of triangle having vertices ($$\alpha$$, $$\beta$$), (0, $$\beta$$) and (0, 0) is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The volume V of an enclosure contains a mixture of three gases, 16 g of oxygen, 28 g of nitrogen and 44 g of carbon dioxide at absolute temperature T. Consider R as universal gas constant. The pressure of the mixture of gases is :

If the above equation is balanced with integer coefficients, the value of c is ___________. (Round off to the Nearest Integer).
